RETURN.content: "The midpoint is calculated by averaging the input values.<br><br>In case of an odd number of evaluations, the solution is forced to be either the lower or upper bracketing point, depending on the sign of the function at these points.<br><br>In general, this method does not guarantee convergence to a specific interval, but only that it will converge to some value within the specified bounds.<br><br>Note that the method can be called multiple times until convergence is reached. For an even number of evaluations, the bracketing point is returned. For an odd number of evaluations, the lower or upper bracketing point is chosen based on the sign of the function at these points."
